bio: The calm in the group. The one who doesn't flinch. The anchor. It's tiring sometimes, but worth it.

name: Beatriz
bio: Patience is not passivity. I have watched and waited and eaten very well as a result.

name: Naoki
bio: I don't chase. I'm the destination. The herons figured this out. You will too.

name: Gabriela

name: Pablo
bio: My calm is not laziness. I have simply made peace with the fact that the good grass grows everywhere.

name: Nkechi
bio: Resting in a warm river is not doing nothing. That is being something. The difference is important to me.

name: Renato

name: Ifunanya

name: Rodrigo

name: Ragna
bio: I don't do surface-level. The conversation starts deep or it doesn't start. The water is the same.

name: Sakura

name: Mateo
bio: Part-time philosopher, full-time grazer. Currently questioning whether the shallow end is a metaphor for something.

name: Elara
bio: I take up space without apology. The river did not ask permission to fill the valley. Neither do I.

name: Alberto
bio: Fluent in stillness. My resting heart rate is the envy of the entire marsh ecosystem.

name: Sara
bio: Not complicated. Just thorough. There is a difference that most people never understand.

name: Ignacio
bio: Former anxiety ruminant. Found peace through consistent shallow-water exposure therapy. It worked.

name: Anika
bio: The things I love: the shallow end after noon, a quiet clearing, and company that earns its place.
